name: Colby Parkinson
headline: Touchdown-fueled 2025 breakout meets a crowded Rams tight end room
body:
Parkinson posted career bests in 2025: 43 grabs on 56 targets for 408 yards and eight touchdowns across 14 games, a 76.8% catch rate that finished him 29th at the position. The efficiency markers were legitimate too, with 0.224 EPA per play, a 64.3% success rate and 1.31 yards per route run, all leaps over his 2024 line. The scoring spike leaned heavily on volume in tight quarters: 22 red-zone looks and 10 end-zone targets fueled those eight scores, a rate unlikely to fully repeat given his modest 9.6% target share and 4.43 air yards per target.

The bigger drag is the depth chart. Tyler Higbee still commands starter snaps, and rookie-level additions Terrance Ferguson and Max Klare crowd the multi-tight-end packages. With an ADP outside the top 220 and only a single-digit target share, Parkinson profiles as a touchdown-dependent stash and in-season streamer rather than a draft-day target. Monitor the role; if the snaps consolidate, he carries genuine spike-week appeal.
